---
title: "Linear Algebra Notes"
---

```{=html}
<section class="course-hero">
  <div>
    <p class="lesson-kicker">Rigorous Saturday-class notes</p>
    <h1>Linear Algebra Notes</h1>
    <p>Distilled lecture notes — symbol dictionaries, theorems formally stated, two independent derivations for key results, verification audits, and the full transcript preserved as appendix. Embedded video for every session.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="course-stats" aria-label="Course statistics">
    <div><strong>28</strong><span>sessions</span></div>
    <div><strong>11</strong><span>lessons distilled</span></div>
  </div>
</section>
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Course scope.** Repository name says *linalg*, but the underlying class covers a broader math arc: modular arithmetic & Fermat–Euler (Sep 2025) → complex exponentials & Euler's formula (Sep 2025) → conic sections (Oct 2025) → polynomial graphing & rational functions (Nov 2025) → series & ζ-function (Dec 2025 – Jan 2026) → linear algebra & eigentheory (Jan – May 2026). Topic tags reflect this; chronological order preserves the spiral teaching pattern.
:::

::: {.callout-warning collapse="true"}
## Page status legend

- **Distilled** — full rigor: symbol dictionary, two independent derivations, verification audit, fragility summary, hand-authored figures, semantically-aligned key frames extracted from video.
- **Stub** — auto-generated from manifest.json metadata: title, lead, topic tags, key theorems list, embedded video, full verbatim transcript appendix. Browsable, searchable, video-playable; but proofs and audits are pending. See [extract_frames.sh](https://github.com/chyj2026/linalg/blob/main/scripts/extract_frames.sh) and [generate_stubs.ps1](https://github.com/chyj2026/linalg/blob/main/scripts/generate_stubs.ps1) for the toolchain.
:::

## Newly distilled lessons

```{=html}
<div class="lesson-grid">
<article class="lesson-card is-new">
  <div class="lesson-card-meta"><span>2026-05-02 — morning</span><span>Published</span></div>
  <h3><a href="lessons/2026-05-02-morning.html">Cayley–Hamilton via Cyclic Basis: Existence Gap and Four Proof Strategies</a></h3>
  <p>Recaps the algebraic Cayley–Hamilton proof using (λI-M)·adj(λI-M) = det(λI-M)·I, then resumes the cyclic-basis proof, identifies the existence-of-cyclic-basis gap, and previews four distinct proof strategies.</p>
</article>
<article class="lesson-card is-new">
  <div class="lesson-card-meta"><span>2026-04-04 — morning</span><span>Published</span></div>
  <h3><a href="lessons/2026-04-04-morning.html">Solving Linear Recursions by Diagonalizing the Companion Matrix; Nilpotent Intro</a></h3>
  <p>Reformulates a second-order linear recursion as first-order vector recursion aₙ₊₁ = M·aₙ, solves by diagonalizing the companion matrix, and introduces the nilpotent block for repeated eigenvalue.</p>
</article>
<article class="lesson-card is-new">
  <div class="lesson-card-meta"><span>2026-04-04 — afternoon (2)</span><span>Published</span></div>
  <h3><a href="lessons/2026-04-04-afternoon-2.html">Powering Jordan Blocks: Binomial Expansion with Nilpotent N</a></h3>
  <p>Continues from morning by explicitly powering (λI+N)^n for 2×2 and generalizing to k×k Jordan blocks, showing that the binomial expansion produces the polynomial-in-n coefficient predicted by the old algebraic theory.</p>
</article>
<article class="lesson-card is-new">
  <div class="lesson-card-meta"><span>2026-03-28 — morning</span><span>Published</span></div>
  <h3><a href="lessons/2026-03-28-morning.html">Gram Matrix VᵀV: Rank Tests, Sub-Volume Inequalities, Gauss-Jordan Inverse</a></h3>
  <p>Uses Gram matrix VᵀV to test linear independence in high-dimensional space (sub-determinants for partial ranks), derives Toby's triangle-inequality constraints on off-diagonal cosines, and teaches Gauss-Jordan row reduction for inverse computation.</p>
</article>
</div>
```

## Full Lesson Library

```{=html}
<div class="lesson-table-wrap">
<table class="lesson-index-table">
  <thead><tr><th>Date</th><th>Session</th><th>Topic</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
<tr><td>2025-09-13</td><td>afternoon</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-09-13-afternoon.html">Modular Arithmetic, Roots of Unity, and Symmetry Counts</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-09-13</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-09-13-morning.html">Fermat's Little Theorem, Euler Totient, and Cyclicity in Modular Arithmetic</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-09-20</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-09-20-morning.html">Proof of Euler's Formula via Additive and Multiplicative Functional Equations</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-09-27</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-09-27-morning.html">Solving cos θ = a and sin θ = a via Complex Exponentials</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-10-04</td><td>afternoon</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-10-04-afternoon.html">Eccentricity Definition of Conics; Parabola as the Borderline Case</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-10-04</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-10-04-morning.html">Hyperbola Geometry: Foci, Asymptotes, and a² + b² = c²</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-10-11</td><td>afternoon</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-10-11-afternoon.html">Cramer's Rule Completed: Existence/Uniqueness Classification via Determinants</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-10-11</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-10-11-morning.html">Cramer's Rule, Cofactors, and Unique Parabola Through Three Points</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-10-18</td><td>afternoon</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-10-18-afternoon.html">Factoring the Quadratic Form ax² + bxy + cy² to Find Asymptotes</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-10-18</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-10-18-morning.html">Quadratic Form Classification of Conics via the Discriminant</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-11-01 <sup title="medium-confidence date">⚠</sup></td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-11-01-morning.html">Graphing Polynomials and Rational Functions: Snaking, Asymptotes, Long Division</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-11-08</td><td>afternoon</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-11-08-afternoon.html">Solving Cubic Equations: Cardano, Vieta, and Complex Cube Roots</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-11-29</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-11-29-morning.html">Asymptotic Analysis: General Principle for Handling Infinitesimals</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-12-06</td><td>afternoon</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-12-06-afternoon.html">Full Graph of a 7th/6th-Degree Rational Function</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2025-12-13</td><td>unspecified</td><td><a href="lessons/2025-12-13.html">Tangent-Circle Chain (AMC 2015 #25): Gateway to Infinite Series and Binary</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-01-03</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-01-03-morning.html">Riemann Zeta: ζ(1+ε) Bound and ζ(2) = π²/6 via sin(x)/x Vieta</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-01-10</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-01-10-morning.html">Linear Recursions with Double Roots: Generating-Function Derivation of n·λ^(n-1)</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-01-24</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-01-24-morning.html">Double-Root Linear Recursion Completed; Generating-Function Identity 1/(1-λx)² = Σ(n+1)λⁿxⁿ</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-02-21</td><td>afternoon</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-02-21-afternoon.html">Skew-Axis Directions for Elliptical Conics via Complex-Rotated Asymptotes (y → yi)</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-02-21 <sup title="medium-confidence date">⚠</sup></td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-02-21-morning.html">Conic Metric Diagonalization Revisited: Trace/Det Invariance, Orthogonal Matrices, and the M·E = E·Λ Eigenvalue Setup</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-02-28 <sup title="unknown date — inferred">⚠</sup></td><td>individual followup</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-02-28-eigenvalues-followup.html">Eigenvalue Equation det(M−λI) = 0 Derived End-to-End; Trace and Determinant from the Characteristic Polynomial</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-03-07</td><td>after — Cramer review</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-03-07-cramer-review.html">Cofactor Inverse Formula Proven; Cramer's Rule Re-derived; Singular Dichotomy</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-03-07</td><td>LVS</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-03-07-LVS.html">Cramer's Rule, Two-Sided Inverse, and Linear Vector Spaces</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-03-28</td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-03-28-morning.html">Gram Matrix VᵀV: Rank Tests, Sub-Volume Inequalities, Gauss-Jordan Inverse</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-04-04</td><td>afternoon (2)</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-04-04-afternoon-2.html">Powering Jordan Blocks: Binomial Expansion with Nilpotent N</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-04-04 <sup title="medium-confidence date">⚠</sup></td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-04-04-morning.html">Solving Linear Recursions by Diagonalizing the Companion Matrix; Nilpotent Intro</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-05-02 <sup title="medium-confidence date">⚠</sup></td><td>morning</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-05-02-morning.html">Cayley–Hamilton via Cyclic Basis: Existence Gap and Four Proof Strategies</a> <span class="mini-badge">Published</span></td></tr>
<tr><td>2026-05-16 <sup title="unknown date — inferred">⚠</sup></td><td>individual (Elaine)</td><td><a href="lessons/2026-05-16-elaine-dotcross.html">Individual Session: Recovering Dot/Cross Product and Shadow-Area Interpretation</a> <span class="mini-badge" style="background:#9ca3af;">Stub</span></td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Excluded duplicate
`Saturday04062026afternoon1.txt` is byte-identical to `Saturday0404morning.txt` — a mislabeled upload. It is recorded in `manifest.json` with `status: "excluded_duplicate"` and is not shown in the index above. The Drive video ID and verbatim transcript are preserved in case the duplicate is informative.

⚠ markers on dates indicate `date_confidence: medium` or `unknown` — verify against Drive `createdTime` or recording metadata before relying on them.
:::
